message:
Fix un-delete bug and add defensive state checks in flush

Bug fixes:
- add() now properly handles un-deleting: removes from pending_delete and
  adds back to pending_new if object was never persisted
- flush DELETE loop now checks tracked.state == Deleted before executing
  (prevents deleting un-deleted objects)
- flush UPDATE loop now checks tracked.state == Persistent before executing
  (defense in depth)

These fixes prevent the scenario where:
1. User loads object from DB
2. User calls delete(obj)
3. User calls add(obj) to un-delete
4. flush() would still DELETE the row (bug - now fixed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
